title: Reditus Space completes first reentry vehicle
summary: Reditus Space, a startup focused on recoverable and reusable spacecraft, has completed its first reentry vehicle, slated for launch later this year.

title: Voyager completes acquisition of Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies has completed its acquisition of Astrobotic Technology, a lunar infrastructure company. The deal follows Astrobotic's recent wins of two NASA lunar lander missions.

title: Getting ready for lunar planetary defense
summary: NASA unveiled plans for a new moon base, marking a significant step toward establishing permanent human presence on the Moon. This development is part of broader efforts to advance space exploration and planetary defense.

title: China lines up methalox Long March 10C as commercial workhorse after first booster recovery
summary: China has designated the methalox Long March 10C as its commercial workhorse after achieving the first successful booster recovery. The move follows an injection of fresh capital into commercial rocket ventures.

title: SpaceX gears up for Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX is preparing for the 13th flight of its Starship rocket, scheduled for July 16, aiming to test fixes from the previous flight and deploy operational Starlink satellites.

title: Parabilis tests propulsion system for maneuverable cubesats
summary: Parabilis Space Technologies has successfully tested a propulsion system designed for maneuverable cubesats. The U.S. Space Force is funding the development of the company's hybrid engine technology for small satellites.

title: FCC approves first Reflect Orbital satellite
summary: The FCC has approved the first Reflect Orbital satellite, which aims to reflect sunlight into nighttime regions. The project has faced criticism from astronomers and environmentalists.

title: Earth observation satellites pass telecom in European space industry sales
summary: European space industry sales rebounded in 2025 after a contraction in 2024, with Earth observation satellites surpassing telecom in sales. This shift is highlighted in Eurospace's latest Facts and Figures report.

title: China unveils members of state-backed commercial space consortium
summary: China has revealed the members of a state-backed commercial space consortium, highlighting key companies deemed as established players by the government. This move provides insight into the country's commercial space sector.

title: The space industry is weighing ambitious hiring against heritage
summary: The space industry is balancing ambitious hiring goals with the preservation of heritage expertise. The challenge lies in maintaining traditional rocket science skills while expanding the workforce.

title: Extremely Large Telescope reaches a major milestone | Space photo of the day for July 13, 2026
summary: The Extremely Large Telescope has achieved a significant milestone, as highlighted in a space photo from July 13, 2026. The telescope's impressive size is prominently featured in the image.

title: Getting Vikram-1 to orbit: Inside Skyroot Aerospace's coming bid to make spaceflight history
summary: Skyroot Aerospace is preparing to launch its Vikram-1 rocket, aiming to revolutionize India's commercial spaceflight industry with a $1.1 billion valuation. The upcoming mission could mark a historic milestone for the company and the country's space sector.

title: Space junk debris cloud discovered in high-traffic orbit 'is a potential minefield' for the costliest satellites
summary: A debris cloud in geosynchronous orbit poses a significant threat to high-value satellites, potentially creating a hazardous environment for critical space assets.

title: Reflect Orbital just got permission to launch its 1st space mirror to orbit. Tens of thousands more could follow
summary: Reflect Orbital has received permission to launch its first space mirror into orbit later this year. Tens of thousands more such mirrors could be deployed over the next decade.

title: Why space games still struggle with the scale of the universe
summary: The article discusses the challenges space games face in accurately representing the vast scale of the universe. It highlights the difficulty of conveying the true enormity of space in gaming.

title: 149 million views! Artemis II moon mission breaks NASA's streaming record
summary: The Artemis II moon mission set a streaming record with 149 million views, marking a significant public engagement milestone for NASA's lunar program.

title: Space medicine breakthrough? Kidney and liver tissue bioprinted off Earth for 1st time ever
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies, a California-based company, successfully bioprinted kidney and liver tissue in space for the first time using a method called bioprinting.

title: 1 month until the total solar eclipse 2026 — Here's what you need to know
summary: The total solar eclipse of 2026 is approaching in one month, and preparations for viewing or traveling to see it are underway. This astronomical event is a significant opportunity for observation and public engagement.

title: Your lost dog can now call home with the world's 1st satellite-connected dog collar
summary: A new dog collar with GPS tracking uses SpaceX's Starlink direct-to-mobile network to provide connectivity from almost anywhere. This innovation allows lost dogs to call home via satellite connection.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 218 — Which Way to the Moonbase?
summary: NASA's new moonbase plans are discussed on Episode 218 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast, featuring planetary scientist Dr. Pascal Lee. The episode explores the challenges and possibilities of establishing a lunar base.

title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope, with support from Webb, has discovered the first stellar-mass black hole in the star cluster Omega Centauri using archival data. This finding marks a significant milestone in understanding black holes within star clusters.

title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
summary: NASA's Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards focus on advanced diagnostics for spacecraft atmospheric entry and autonomous navigation using machine learning. The awards support innovative research to enhance spacecraft guidance, navigation, and control systems.

title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
summary: NASA volunteers have helped Zooniverse, a NASA grantee, reach 1 billion classifications through their online research platform. This milestone highlights the contributions of volunteers worldwide in analyzing astronomical data.

title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
summary: NASA photographers captured unique aerial images of the agency's missions using an F-18 over Washington. These photos provide rare behind-the-scenes views of NASA's research and operations.

title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
summary: The image shows the waxing gibbous moon from the International Space Station, 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar on June 26, 2026. This phase occurs before the full moon and is characterized by the moon's increasing brightness.

title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
summary: Radar data from the NISAR satellite revealed significant ground displacement in La Guaira and nearby areas due to the June 2026 earthquakes in Venezuela. This highlights the satellite's capability to monitor seismic activity and its impacts.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, joining Expedition 74 to advance scientific research. The mission includes cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, lifting off at 10:47 a.m. EDT.

title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
summary: NASA's IXPE has directly measured the magnetic fields of the pulsar PSR J1101−6101 within the Lighthouse Nebula, offering new insights into extreme cosmic objects.

title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
summary: Reports evaluating Umbra's Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data highlight its contributions to Earth science research. The findings underscore the growing role of commercial data in scientific applications.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines, marking a significant milestone in its development. The test demonstrates progress toward future missions.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This development highlights the ongoing revitalization of the region's space industry infrastructure.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station is part of Vast's broader ambitions in low Earth orbit commercialization.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support the development of landers for future lunar missions.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent New Glenn anomaly that caused significant damage. The company aims to restore the launch site efficiently after the incident.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to perform multiple tasks. The innovation is positioned as a significant advancement in space robotics technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, supported by young researchers, tests and validates materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for mission success. The team focuses on assembling spacecraft with non-ordinary materials to guarantee durability.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its scheduled debut flight this fall.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to launch an in-orbit mirror, despite concerns about its environmental and astronomical impact. The FCC stated that such concerns fall outside its regulatory scope.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for satellite power beaming. The company specializes in laser systems capable of transmitting energy to other satellites in orbit.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the feasibility of building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective security and space-based defense capabilities.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift away from Jeff Bezos' personal funding. The move signals the company's transition to a more independent financial model.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This achievement highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, targeting launches no earlier than 2030. The Japanese company is offering a new lunar integrator service to facilitate payload delivery to the Moon.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. This investment reflects growing industry interest in the more efficient rocket propulsion method.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision last week.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight from orbit back to Earth. This marks a step toward potential solar energy applications.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully demonstrated direct-to-device (D2D) SMS and SOS messaging on the Thuraya-4 satellite. The partners announced the test results on July 9 and anticipate future rollouts.

title: Ovzon’s GEO Connectivity and Terminals Complete Arctic Tests
summary: Ovzon has successfully completed Arctic trials to test its GEO connectivity and terminals in extreme environments. The company conducted three High North expeditions to validate its technology.

title: Simera Sense to Supply Imagers for Canary Islands Satellite Constellation
summary: Simera Sense will supply multispectral imagers for the Canary Islands satellite constellation project. Telespazio Ibérica has selected Simera Sense for this initiative.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is expanding its Earth Observation (EO) capabilities by acquiring a majority stake in French analytics firm CLS. This vertical integration move aims to enhance MDA Space's data processing and insight generation in the EO sector.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has submitted a filing with the FCC to launch a third-generation satellite constellation consisting of 100,000 satellites for high-speed internet services. The Gen3 constellation aims to provide multi-gigabit connectivity using W- and D-band frequencies.

title: Telesat to Provide Mil-Ka Arctic Connectivity for Canada’s ESCP-P Program
summary: Telesat has secured a contract with the Canadian government to supply Military Ka-band satellite capacity for Canada's Enhanced Satellite Communications Project – Polar (ESCP-P) program. The agreement aims to enhance Arctic connectivity for military and government use.

title: Rivada Signs Connectivity Partner in Nepal
summary: Rivada Space Networks has partnered with a telecoms company in Nepal to provide connectivity services. The deal involves Mercantile Space, a communications technology firm in the country.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have joined the competition for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 awards. The field of eligible companies for these awards has now expanded to seven.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, solidifying its role in Europe's launch supply chain. This deal marks one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.
